message:
The other night, we had a surprise blackout—just when I thought I’d settled the kids in for a cozy movie night. Suddenly, the TV went dark, and I could feel the wave of disappointment wash over them. 

In a flash, I rummaged around for our trusty flashlights and a portable power bank to keep the phones charged (because let’s be honest, that’s a must). The kids were a mix of giggles and grumbles, but I decided to turn it into an adventure. 

We set up a little lantern in the living room, and I started telling them stories from my childhood—yes, the ones where we had to entertain ourselves without screens! They loved it, and soon enough, the initial grumpiness turned into laughter and fun. 

It reminded me that with just a bit of prep, we can turn unexpected moments into memories. Plus, a little flashlight glow can make any room feel magical! 

Have you ever had to get creative during a power outage? What did you do to keep the fun going?
